title: lilium bulbiferum subsp. davuricum
intro: Lilium bulbiferum subsp. davuricum, also known as the Tiger Lily, is a subspecies of the lily plant native to the mountainous regions of Asia and Russia. It is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional Chinese and Russian medicine, where it is used to treat a range of health issues including inflammation, fever, and respiratory problems. The Tiger Lily is rich in flavonoids and other bioactive compounds that have been shown to have antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antibacterial properties, providing health benefits such as reduced oxidative stress, improved immune function, and protection against infections. Traditionally, the plant is used in herbal preparations such as infusions, tinctures, and ointments, often combined with other herbs like ginger and ginseng to enhance its medicinal effects.
